name: CSV Export
reasonAbandoned: Users preferred direct dashboard downloads. The export wizard had a 74% abandonment rate at Step 2.
lessonsLearned: Multi-step flows need progress indicators and save-draft capability
Validate demand with a fake door test before building the full wizard
Export formats should match the tools users already use (Excel, not CSV)

name: In-App Chat
reasonAbandoned: Users had established Slack workflows. No pull from the market; feature was a solution looking for a problem.
lessonsLearned: Don't compete with entrenched communication tools
Integration with existing tools (Slack, Teams) would have cost 10% of the effort
Conduct Jobs-to-be-Done interviews before committing to large communication features

name: Dark Mode
reasonAbandoned: Shipped but never promoted. Adoption stalled due to poor discoverability — no in-app announcement or onboarding nudge.
lessonsLearned: Shipping is not enough; features need active promotion pipelines
Add feature announcements to onboarding flows and changelog emails
A/B test feature visibility in settings vs. contextual prompt

name: Legacy Reports
reasonAbandoned: Superseded by a new dashboard builder but never formally deprecated. Maintained in parallel for 3 years at significant engineering cost.
lessonsLearned: Every feature needs a planned deprecation lifecycle from day one
Sunset timelines should be communicated 90 days in advance
Running parallel systems doubles maintenance cost without doubling value

name: API Webhooks
lessonsLearned: Developer-facing features reward investment — power users become champions
Good API documentation is as important as the feature itself
Adoption grew 3x after adding a webhook tester UI to reduce setup friction
